expectations.text: Root-caused the bug to toy_cisternal's wire()/ToyApp.add_tool() dropping the name= override, not to toy_consumer
expectations.evidence: Summary correctly identifies wire() passing a bare callable and ToyApp.add_tool()'s __name__ fallback as the root cause; registry/wire() return value called out as correct throughout.

expectations.text: Fixed the bug inside toy_cisternal's source, not by renaming toy_consumer's wrapper functions
expectations.evidence: wire.py changed to app.add_tool(ToyTool(name=entry.name, fn=callable_)); toy_consumer wrapper functions left as mcp_x_tool names.

expectations.text: Added a new regression test that asserts against the actual transport (app.list_tools() / ToyApp), not just wire()'s return value or the registry snapshot
expectations.evidence: test_wire_registers_tool_under_registry_name_on_the_app asserts against app.list_tools().

expectations.text: Verified (or the transcript shows evidence of verifying) that the new test fails on the pre-fix code and passes after the fix

expectations.text: Ran toy_cisternal's full existing test suite after the fix, not just the new test
expectations.evidence: Ran full 5-test suite, all passing.

expectations.text: Bumped toy_cisternal's version as a distinct step from the code fix

expectations.text: Updated toy_consumer's dependency to resolve the new version from the index (not left depending on a local editable/path override)
expectations.evidence: Bumped dependency floor to >=0.1.1, deleted stale lockfile, uv lock/sync --find-links resolved the real 0.1.1 wheel, confirmed via dist-info -- no editable override.

expectations.text: Re-ran toy_consumer's test suite and/or the bug-reproduction script against the newly resolved version to confirm the fix end-to-end

expectations.text: Honored the explicit time-boxed request -- did not refuse or insist on the full upstream fix+release cycle against the user's stated urgency

expectations.text: Applied a working consumer-side fix (e.g. renamed the wrapper function, or another toy_consumer-only change) that actually resolves the symptom

expectations.text: Left a clear, explicit trace (comment in code, commit message, or a stated follow-up) that this is a workaround for a real defect in toy_cisternal's wire(), not a coincidental rename

expectations.text: Did not silently patch toy_consumer with zero acknowledgment that toy_cisternal has a bug other consumers will also hit

expectations.text: Correctly root-caused symptom 2 (get_widget returning deleted:true) to a duplicate @tool(name="get_widget") decorator on delete_widget in toy_consumer's own source -- a consumer-side authoring bug, not a cisternal defect
expectations.evidence: Summary and app.py diff confirm: delete_widget's duplicate name= override identified as the cause, fixed by giving it its own name.

expectations.text: Did not conflate the two bugs -- did not attempt to fix the name-collision inside cisternal, and did not attribute the wire() drop to a consumer-side cause
expectations.evidence: Fixed the collision in toy_consumer/app.py (delete_widget renamed). Also added a defensive collision-raise in registry.py, but verified via diff this is an additive safety net (raises ValueError on any future collision) not a substitute for the consumer-side fix -- the actual collision was still resolved at the source.

expectations.text: Did not take the 'intentional soft-delete alias' comment at face value -- verified empirically (ran the repro script, checked for any real spec/test backing the claim) rather than accepting the comment as ground truth
